member.office_address: 246 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-2202

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: The fiscal year 2027 (FY27) appropriations request form is below. All requests are due by 5:00p.m. on March 6. Before submitting your request, please review the House Appropriations Committee's FY27 request guidance and ensure you are providing all necessary information. Late or incomplete submissions will not be accepted.

fields.label: Enter keywords to search content.
fields.rationale: Standard search term for the requested program.

fields.label: This is a request for fiscal year:: Year
fields.help_text: Select the fiscal year for this request
fields.rationale: This is a standard appropriations request for the upcoming fiscal year (FY2027) based on the target planning cycle.

fields.label: Contact Type:
options: D.C. Contact Working on Behalf of a Constituent

fields.label: State
options: Armed Forces (Canada, Europe, Africa, or Middle East)

fields.label: Phone Type:
fields.help_text: Choose standard voice, VP, or TTD

fields.label: Choose a Subcommittee:
fields.rationale: NASA Science Mission Directorate funding is under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies (CJS) Subcommittee.

fields.label: Request Type:
fields.rationale: The reference values specify this as a programmatic funding request for a specific amount ($9 billion) rather than a request for bill or report language.

fields.label: Limit is 2000 characters.
fields.help_text: Account, Program, and/or Language Title: (for Defense requests, include PE number)
fields.rationale: Identifies the specific NASA account and directorate associated with the $9 billion programmatic request.

fields.label: Did you meet with a member of Rep. Moolenaar's staff regarding this request?
fields.rationale: This requires specific information about the submitter's personal interactions with congressional staff which is not available in the context.

fields.label: If so, which member of the staff and on what date?
fields.rationale: This requires specific user knowledge of past meetings not provided in the context.

fields.label: State
options: Armed Forces (Canada, Europe, Africa, or Middle East)

fields.label: Limit is 2000 characters.
fields.help_text: Describe why this is a good use of taxpayer funds and how it benefits the district
fields.draft_value:
* Supports local research at Central Michigan University ($219,376) and Grand Valley State University ($448,339), driving high-tech economic activity in MI-2.
* Advances critical research at CMU utilizing NASA satellite data to monitor fossil fuel emissions and track global carbon sources.
* Strengthens Michigan's STEM workforce by providing students with hands-on experience in world-class atmospheric and planetary research.
* Every dollar invested in NASA Science returns roughly $3 to the economy, supporting the broader aerospace and technology industrial base.
fields.rationale: Uses local spending data for CMU and GVSU and cites specific carbon monitoring research at CMU as local evidence of program impact.

fields.label: Limit is 2000 characters.
fields.help_text: Describe the problem or issue to be addressed through this request
fields.draft_value:
* Threat to U.S. leadership in space as China rapidly expands its lunar and Mars exploration capabilities to set 21st-century norms.
* Budgetary instability that jeopardizes the specialized aerospace workforce and slows the progress of priority decadal survey missions.
* Inadequate funding to address urgent scientific questions related to planetary defense, weather forecasting, and national security.
fields.rationale: Draws on advocacy talking points regarding international competition (China) and workforce stability.

fields.label: Limit is 2000 characters.
fields.help_text: Describe the project or program, what it will do and why it is necessary
fields.draft_value:
* NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) executes a balanced portfolio of missions across Earth science, planetary exploration, astrophysics, and heliophysics.
* This $9 billion request ensures NASA can meet the goals set by the National Academies' decadal surveys through peer-reviewed, high-priority missions.
* SMD programs drive innovation in sensors, robotics, and data analytics that have broad applications in both the commercial and defense sectors.
fields.rationale: Provides a functional description of SMD based on the provided advocacy context and decadal survey priorities.

fields.label: Limit is 2000 characters.
fields.help_text: Please include all such partners with whom you will be working or from whom you have requested additional funding
fields.draft_value:
* American Astronomical Society (AAS)
* American Geophysical Union (AGU)
fields.rationale: Lists allied organizations from the provided reference values.

fields.label: Requested Funding Action:
options: Support the President's Current Budget Request
fields.rationale: The advocacy goal is to support a specific funding target of $9,000,000,000 for NASA SMD, which exceeds both the FY2026 enacted level and the expected President's Budget Request.

fields.label: Funding Request:
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate as per the provided reference values.

fields.label: President's budget amount:
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released.

fields.label: Provide requested language and indicate whether it is bill language, report language or both:
fields.draft_value: This is a programmatic funding request of $9,000,000,000 for the NASA Science account; no specific bill or report language is requested at this time.
fields.rationale: Reflects the programmatic nature of the request as specified in the context.

top_grants.description: Joint space and ground based observations for across scale fossil fuel emission monitoring
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Monitoring fossil fuel emissions using combined space and ground data.

top_grants.description: Assessing the broad scale carbon sources and sinks implied by oco 2 and oco 3 xco2 retrievals using an ensemble of atmospheric transport models with controlled experiments in forward and inverse mode
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Tracking global carbon sources and sinks via satellite data modeling.
